column5: Epic: Critical Error Handling

column4: Global Error Handling on All Screen Load Functions
column5: As a user, I want every screen to recover gracefully from network failures, so that I never see an infinite loading spinner with no escape.
column12:
AC1: Given any of these 10 screens is loading data: Home, Discover, Messages, Matches, Profile, Feed, Activity, Goals, Leaderboard, Challenges — When a Supabase query fails or network is unavailable, Then the ErrorState component (from FM-103) is shown with 'Try Again' button

AC2: Given the ErrorState is displayed on a screen, When the user taps 'Try Again', Then the load() function re-invokes, the ErrorState disappears, and the skeleton loader appears

AC3: Given a retry also fails, When the error occurs again, Then the ErrorState shows again (no infinite retry loop, no infinite skeleton)

AC4: Given a screen uses pull-to-refresh (RefreshControl), When the refresh fails, Then the existing data stays on screen (NOT replaced by ErrorState), and a toast shows 'Could not refresh. Please try again.'

AC5: Given any screen's load() function, When inspecting the code, Then every Promise.all is wrapped in try/catch, errors are logged with screen name prefix (e.g. '[Home] load failed'), and setLoading(false) is in a finally block

AC6: Given a skeleton loader is displayed, When 15 seconds pass without data or error, Then the ErrorState is shown automatically (timeout fallback)
column13:
QA must verify error state on ALL 10 screens:
? 1. Home (app/(tabs)/home.tsx)
? 2. Discover (app/(tabs)/discover.tsx)
? 3. Messages (app/(tabs)/messages.tsx)
? 4. Matches (app/(tabs)/matches.tsx)
? 5. Profile (app/(tabs)/profile.tsx)
? 6. Feed (app/(tabs)/feed.tsx)
? 7. Activity (app/(tabs)/activity.tsx)
? 8. Goals (app/(tabs)/goals.tsx)
? 9. Leaderboard (app/(tabs)/leaderboard.tsx)
? 10. Challenges (app/(tabs)/challenges.tsx)

Per screen test steps:
• Turn on airplane mode ? open screen ? verify ErrorState shows
• Tap 'Try Again' with network restored ? verify data loads
• Pull-to-refresh with network off ? verify existing data stays + toast appears
• Verify no console errors or unhandled promise rejections
• Verify skeleton loader never shows longer than 15 seconds

column4: React Error Boundary in Root Layout
column5: As a user, I want the app to show a recovery screen instead of crashing, so that I can continue using the app after an unexpected error.
column12:
AC1: Given any unhandled JavaScript error occurs in the component tree, When the error propagates to the root layout, Then a full-screen error view shows with 'Something went wrong' and a 'Restart' button

AC2: Given the error screen is displayed, When I tap 'Restart', Then the error boundary resets and the app re-renders to the last working screen

AC3: Given any crash scenario, When the error screen appears, Then no raw error text, stack traces, or technical jargon are visible to the user

AC4: Given a network error (not a JS crash), When a screen's load() fails, Then the Error Boundary does NOT trigger — individual screen ErrorState handles it instead

AC5: Given the ErrorState component is created, When it renders, Then it uses theme colors (useTheme), accepts onRetry callback, and shows icon + message + 'Try Again' button
column13:
• Trigger a deliberate JS error by adding `throw new Error('test')` in a component render — verify boundary catches it
• Verify restart button recovers the app to Home screen in usable state
• Verify error is logged to console (console.error) for debugging
• Verify no user-facing technical jargon in the error screen
• Verify network errors do NOT trigger the error boundary (they use ErrorState instead)
• Verify ErrorState component renders correctly in BOTH dark and light themes
• Verify ErrorState 'Try Again' button has loading state while retrying

column5: Epic: Data Safety & Destructive Actions
As a user, I want 'Delete messages' and 'Unmatch' to be completely separate actions with confirmation, so that I never accidentally lose a connection.
column12:
AC1: Given I swipe left on a conversation and tap 'Delete messages', When the confirmation dialog appears, Then it says 'Delete all messages with [Name]? The match will be preserved.' and only deletes messages, not the match

AC2: Given I swipe left on a conversation and tap 'Unmatch', When the confirmation dialog appears, Then it says 'Unmatch [Name]? This will permanently remove this connection and all messages. This cannot be undone.'

AC3: Given I tap 'Cancel' on either confirmation dialog, When the dialog closes, Then no data is changed and the conversation remains as-is

AC4: Given I confirm 'Unmatch', When the action completes, Then the match status is set to 'unmatched' (distinct from 'declined')
column13:
• Verify 'Delete messages' only removes messages, match row preserved
• Verify 'Unmatch' sets status='unmatched' not 'declined'
• Verify both actions show confirmation dialog before executing
• Verify Cancel on dialog makes no data changes
• Verify swipe actions are clearly labeled and visually distinct

column4: Account Deletion with Full Auth Cleanup
column5: As a user, I want account deletion to fully remove my data including auth credentials, so that I cannot log back in to a broken state.
column12:
AC1: Given I tap 'Delete Account' in Settings, When the first dialog appears, Then it asks 'Are you sure? This permanently deletes all your data.'

AC2: Given I confirm the first dialog, When the second confirmation appears, Then I must type 'DELETE' to proceed

AC3: Given I confirm both dialogs, When the Edge Function executes, Then it deletes: profile row, push tokens, AND auth.users entry via auth.admin.deleteUser()

AC4: Given deletion succeeds, When I try to log in with the same email, Then authentication fails with 'No account found'

AC5: Given deletion fails (network error), When the error is shown, Then the Delete button re-enables and I can try again

• Verify two-step confirmation (dialog + type DELETE)
• Verify Edge Function deletes profile, tokens, and auth user
• Verify re-login with deleted email fails
• Verify delete button re-enables on failure (not stuck disabled)

column5: Epic: Streak & Trust Mechanics
As a user, I want my streak to accurately reflect consecutive workout days, so that I can trust the number I see on my profile.
column12:
AC1: Given I logged a workout yesterday (local time), When I log a workout today, Then my streak increments by 1

AC2: Given I logged a workout 2+ days ago, When I log a workout today, Then my streak resets to 1

AC3: Given I already logged a workout today, When I try to log again, Then my streak does NOT increment (stays the same)

AC4: Given any streak update, When the operation executes, Then it uses a Supabase RPC function (atomic, not read-modify-write) and all date comparisons use local calendar dates (not UTC)
column13:
• Verify consecutive day logging increments streak correctly (test 3 consecutive days)
• Verify 2-day gap resets streak to 1
• Verify same-day duplicate does not inflate streak
• Verify late-night workout (11pm) counts for correct local day, not UTC next day

column5: As a user, I want completed-session stats to update fairly, so that one person confirming doesn't credit both without consent.
column12:
AC1: Given User A confirms a session, When the confirmation saves, Then only User A's confirmed_by flag is set (not both users' stats)

AC2: Given both User A and User B have confirmed, When the system checks, Then sessions_completed increments for both users

AC3: Given User A reports no-show, When the report saves, Then only User B's sessions_no_show increments (not User A's)

AC4: Given any stat update, When it executes, Then it uses an atomic Supabase RPC (not read-modify-write)
column13:
• Verify one-sided confirmation only updates confirming user's flag
• Verify stats only increment when both users have confirmed
• Verify no-show does not penalize the reporter
• Verify concurrent confirmations don't lose data (race condition test)

AC3: Given the Edge Function, When it runs, Then it uses the service-role key and verifies the caller's is_admin status from the database
